title: The MACU Report — Episode 22 — "Cut With Sunshine"

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Cold-start fire-and-poll. For each cue with non-empty vo: POST the line (multipart form-data -F, NOT JSON) to the speaker_map voice -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. HOLD cues (empty vo + hold_seconds): silent wav, no subtitle; hold_style 'play' = PLAY the clip. Per-shot screen time = cue duration / number of shots. Jank filtergraph, RIFE 3x, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, mix sfx, burn subs -> final/<slug>.mp4.

title_assets.cut_with_sunshine: episodes/ep-022/titles/cut_with_sunshine.mp4 (NEW — Hyperframes house-style CUT WITH SUNSHINE card; flag for build)

subtitles.note: Do NOT put FontName=/Fontsize= in force_style; stage 8 prepends its own. HOLD cues have empty vo => no subtitle by design.

ron.core: an earnest disheveled 1970s television news anchor in a rumpled suit at a news desk, studio lights

walter.core: a calm distinguished elderly 1970s television news co-anchor at a news desk, measured gravitas

sweet.core: a smooth calm wasteland dealer in a pristine pale apron over a collared shirt, holding a paper packet of powder like a sommelier, a faint knowing smile, behind a folding table stacked with paper packets

goldtooth.core: a flashy wasteland dentist in a fur-collared coat over dental scrubs, a single gold front tooth, gold rings, big grin

roscoe.core: a hulking wasteland bouncer in a tight muscle shirt and dark sunglasses, arms crossed, gold chain, theatrical posing

daryl.core: a strung-out wasteland regular, gaunt, wide-eyed, twitchy, clutching a sweating paper cup

warner.core: a sober earnest gaunt man in a frayed coat holding up a paper packet, reading the label, urgent

broll.crystal_packets: a pyramid of plain paper drink-mix packets on a folding table at dusk
broll.lemonade_stand: a child's wooden lemonade stand re-purposed as a wasteland dealing corner, a hand-lettered CRYSTAL sign
broll.ron_mug: a television news anchor's coffee mug full of pale cloudy lemonade on a news desk, a torn paper packet beside it
broll.crystal_pitcher: a sweating glass pitcher of pale lemonade with a ladle, condensation, on a news desk
broll.cut_lab: a grimy back-room table where pale powder is cut with ash and sand, scales, a single hanging bulb
broll.glass_water: a plain glass of clear water on an empty news desk
broll.stride_watch: a sleek minimalist smart-watch on a wrist, the round face glowing soft friendly white, a step count on the display, dark background
broll.safe: a vintage wooden console cabinet with a single round glowing glass lens, a cozy 1950s living room, soft lamplight
broll.everwell: an upright pale-wood casket pod with a single round glowing dial in a softly lit funeral showroom
broll.vending_machine: a battered glass-front vending machine with a glowing dial face, an empty slot, in an empty concrete room

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, MACU! Ron here, feeling GREAT, feeling sharp, hydrated, locked in. Big news night and I am READY for it. What a beverage this morning. What a beverage.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And I'm Walter. Ron is hydrated. Ron is very hydrated. Whatever that is, it is not water, and it is not my business.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: It's lemon water, Walter. It's a wellness thing. It's for the segment.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Newscaster's fatigue. It happens to the best of us. Anyway — the news. And the news, like Ron, is mostly about beverages tonight. Before we begin: a man has been knocking on the inside of the vending machine since Thursday. Maintenance is aware. The machine is aware.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: To the markets. Overnight, crystal overtook teeth as the settlement's leading currency. Tooth futures fell. Crystal futures rose. The economists have stopped explaining things and simply point at the table. Two of them have also joined the line. For research.

cues.speaker: SWEET
cues.vo: Demand's healthy. We don't sell to everyone — we serve the thirsty. A serving's a pour. A regular's a friend. We don't push. We don't have to. The thirst does the pushing for us.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: VOLUME, baby! My cousin Sweet moved more pallets last NIGHT than I moved teeth all YEAR! And the BEAUTIFUL part? It comes BACK. A tooth, you pull it ONCE. Crystal? They finish the packet and they come back THIRSTY. That ain't a sale, baby, that's a SUBSCRIPTION. I shoulda been in beverages my WHOLE life.

cues.speaker: ROSCOE
cues.vo: This SUPPLY is SACRED, brother! You skim from Sweet's stock, you become PART of the INVENTORY! The last man who watered down the product? We watered down HIM! He's THIRTY PERCENT himself now! He floats! He don't sink no more, he just FLOATS, OHHH YEAHHH!

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Jesus.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: But success breeds danger. Tonight, an exposé. The crystal on your corner may not be pure. Unscrupulous dealers are cutting it — with ash, with grit, with floor. The settlement is, understandably, devastated.

cues.speaker: DARYL
cues.vo: Man, I got a bad batch. I poured it and it was, like — GRAY. And gritty. It tasted like a DRIVEWAY. And I drank the whole thing? Because I'd already committed? That's the disease talking, man. And the disease has OPINIONS now.

cues.speaker: SWEET
cues.vo: Whoever's cutting my product with the ground is going to have a very calm conversation with me. It's sugar-free. There is no reason to add anything. That's the part that should scare you. Pure is the whole point. Pure is free of everything you'd add to it.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: So if you're buying, MACU, buy clean. Buy from a name you trust. Buy from — well. I'm not allowed to say. But he's got a folding table and a very calm voice.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Ron, you've just had a fourth.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That was the SAME one, Walter. I've been nursing it. I'm a nurser. Let's go to a sponsor.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Hydration streak: four days, Ron! A personal best. You haven't felt this good since — well. You haven't felt this good. Heart rate: elevated. Cause: unknown. Logging as joy. Keep it up.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Yeah. Keep it up.

cues.speaker: THE VENDOR
cues.vo: Shopping hour, MACU. Today's special: nothing, because you already want the one thing. Insert tribute. Today's tribute is one packet. You know the one. Place it in the slot. Thank you. The machine is thirsty too. We are all thirsty now. Together. Forever. Your packet has been accepted. You may keep the machine company. Ding.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And the forecast. A bright day — cloudless, dry, a high of seventy-three. So dry, in fact, the settlement is advised to stay indoors and drink plenty of fluids, which, frankly, was already the plan. Cut with sunshine, the meteorologists are calling it. Visibility is excellent. We can see everyone. Everyone can see us. Condition rating: extremely mild.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's our broadcast, MACU. A booming trade, a purity crisis, and a community that just wants something clean to drink. I should know. Buy responsibly. Buy a lot, but responsibly.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Sweet. Tomorrow. Can we talk about — a steadier supply. For the show. I can't keep running to the corner mid-broadcast, it looks unprofessional.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Anyway. Goodnight, MACU — stay safe, stay strong, and good luck. And drink clean.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Five days tomorrow, Ron. Nobody's ever made it to five. ...I believe in you.
